title: The AI Compute Crunch Is Here (and It's Affecting the Entire Economy)
summary: Venture capitalists can't subsidize cheap AI forever, and the hunger for more compute is affecting the labor market, the gadget market, and electricity prices.
reason: The AI compute crunch exposes the extraction mechanism directly: venture capital subsidy masks the real cost of enclosure, and when the subsidy ends, those costs route onto workers, consumers, and the electrical grid — classic commons capture made visible.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to trace where compute costs are actually flowing (labor market, energy prices, consumer hardware) when the VC alibi of cheap AI dissolves, revealing who bears the real cost of the enclosure.

title: Book bans and culture wars came for libraries. They’re still standing strong.

title: ‘Athens cannot operate as a giant hotel’: mayor vows to rescue capital from overtourism
summary: Haris Doukas warns that with 700,000 residents and 8 million tourists, people are being pushed out of their neighbourhoods In the heart of ancient Athens, on narrow streets and around archaeological sites, visitor groups appear to be everywhere, snaking their way behind tour guides. At other times, officials would have welcomed such scenes. But for Haris Doukas, the socialist mayor who is determined to reclaim the capital’s congested city centre for its citizens, the start of the tourist season leaves much of its historic heart at risk of “over-saturation.” Entire neighbourhoods, he believes, are in danger of losing their authenticity because of uncontrolled tourist development. Continue reading...
